evals.prompt: Implement a REST API for user management in NestJS. Endpoints: POST /users (register), GET /users (list with pagination+search), GET /users/:id, PATCH /users/:id, DELETE /users/:id. Include DTOs with validation, proper error responses, and Swagger docs.
evals.expected_output: NestJS module with controller, service, DTOs, validation, error handling, and Swagger decorators

assertions.name: pagination
assertions.description: List endpoint supports page/limit with meta response

assertions.name: error_responses
assertions.description: NotFoundException for missing users, ConflictException for duplicates

evals.prompt: Implement rate limiting on our NestJS API. Global limit: 100 requests/minute. Auth endpoints: 10 requests/minute. Use @nestjs/throttler. Return 429 with Retry-After header when exceeded.
evals.expected_output: Throttler configuration with global and per-route limits, proper 429 response

assertions.name: auth_limit
assertions.description: Auth endpoints limited to 10 requests/minute via decorator

evals.prompt: Implement a file upload endpoint in NestJS. Accept multipart form data, validate file type (jpg/png/pdf only) and size (max 10MB), upload to S3, and return the file URL. Use Multer and AWS SDK.
evals.expected_output: File upload endpoint with validation, S3 integration, and URL response

assertions.name: type_validation
assertions.description: Validates file MIME type (not just extension)

assertions.name: s3_upload
assertions.description: Uploads to S3 bucket with proper key naming

assertions.name: url_response
assertions.description: Returns the S3 file URL in response

evals.prompt: Add request/response logging middleware to our NestJS API. Log: method, URL, status code, response time, user ID (from JWT), and request ID. Use structured JSON. Exclude health check and Swagger endpoints.
evals.expected_output: NestJS middleware or interceptor with structured logging and exclusions

assertions.name: structured_json
assertions.description: Logs as JSON objects, not string concatenation

assertions.name: response_timing
assertions.description: Measures and logs response time in ms

assertions.name: user_id
assertions.description: Extracts user ID from JWT payload

evals.prompt: Implement a webhook endpoint that receives Stripe events. Verify the signature using Stripe SDK, handle checkout.session.completed and invoice.payment_failed events, and ensure idempotency (processing same event twice is safe).
evals.expected_output: Webhook controller with signature verification, event handling, and idempotency check

assertions.name: event_routing
assertions.description: Routes to handler based on event type

assertions.name: idempotency
assertions.description: Checks if event was already processed (by event ID)

assertions.name: raw_body
assertions.description: Uses raw body for signature verification, not parsed JSON
